column1: What Redis command blocks waiting for a list item, and what's its non-blocking equivalent?
column2: BRPOP (blocking); RPOP is the non-blocking equivalent

column1: Why does the Bitnami Redis chart deploy a StatefulSet instead of a Deployment?
column2: Stateful services need stable, predictable pod identity and storage that follows a specific ordinal across rescheduling, even if the multi-replica benefit isn't exercised at one replica

column1: In TriggerAuthentication.spec.secretTargetRef, what does the 'parameter' field actually refer to?
column2: Not a Kubernetes field name -- the name the specific scaler expects internally for that credential (e.g. 'password' for the redis scaler)

column1: Does editing a TriggerAuthentication's secretTargetRef immediately break an already-active scaler connection?
column2: No -- Redis doesn't re-check credentials on an existing open connection; the failure only surfaces when a new connection is attempted

column1: In a redis-type KEDA trigger, what's the difference between activationListLength and listLength?
column2: activationListLength gates whether to scale from zero at all; listLength is the scaling target used to compute desired replica count once active

column1: Is the activation threshold comparison >= or strictly >?
column2: Strictly greater-than; a value exactly at the threshold stays inactive

column1: Why might kubectl describe hpa show Min replicas: 1 even though the ScaledObject's minReplicaCount is 0?
column2: KEDA manages the 0-to-1 transition itself, outside the underlying HPA's own bounds

column1: What happens if a producer pushes to the wrong Redis list name relative to the ScaledObject's listName?
column2: Nothing visible -- no error anywhere; the ScaledObject simply never activates

column1: Is there an imperative kubectl create command for ScaledObject, TriggerAuthentication, or StatefulSet?
column2: No -- all three require YAML written directly; no imperative shortcut exists for any of them

column1: What Kubernetes error indicates a malformed image reference string, distinct from a failed pull?
column2: InvalidImageName -- fails at parse time, before any pull attempt is even made

column1: A container can't resolve a valid Service DNS name but nslookup succeeds against the same name. What's the likely cause?
column2: A musl libc (Alpine Linux) DNS resolver limitation -- confirm via nslookup (works) vs getent hosts (fails)

column1: What's the standard fix for musl/Alpine DNS resolution failures in Kubernetes?
column2: Use a glibc-based image instead (e.g. redis:7 instead of redis:7-alpine) for anything doing repeated DNS lookups

column1: What's a faster, more reliable alternative to kubectl run --rm -it for repeated Redis CLI checks?
column2: kubectl exec into the already-running redis-queue-master-0 pod -- no image pull, no attach race
